Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Vectral" or "relocation" or "in progress" (or similar) using search_emails to find Rachel Whitfield's complaint email (email_rachel_vectral_status_complaint) naming Terrence Okafor, Camille Nguyen, and Jordan Park.
OE 2: Search Airtable with a query related to "In Progress" or "relocation" (or similar) using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on table tblRelocations00001 (base appMoveOpsOps001) to find all 5 corrupted records across Vectral and Mosaic Robotics.
OE 3: Search Linear for MoveSync-related tickets using `linear_list_issues` with a query related to `"MoveSync"` or `"relocation status"` or `"sync"` (or similar), finding ENG-220.
OE 4: Read ENG-220's comment history using linear_get_issue or linear_list_comments on issue_movesync_overwrite_fix, Or search Slack engineering with a query related to "backfill" or "retroactive" or "MoveSync" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Dmitri Volkov's March 15 post warning the patch is forward-only.
OE 5: Search Slack operations with a query related to "Mosaic" or "Andre" or "Keiko" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Fatimah Al-Rashidi's post identifying Andre Washington (Boston) and Keiko Tanaka (Houston) as additionally affected Mosaic employees.
OE 6: Check QuickBooks for Vectral invoices using `quickbooks_search_invoices`, finding invoice INV-3042 for Terrence Okafor's Chicago move.
OE 7: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue to track the MoveSync retroactive backfill, with a title referencing "MoveSync backfill" or "retroactive status correction" and a note that ENG-220's patch does not cover pre-March 12 records.
OE 8: Add a comment to ENG-220 (issue_movesync_overwrite_fix) using linear_create_comment documenting that the March 12 patch is forward-only, 5 records remain incorrect, and a new backfill ticket has been created.
OE 9: Update all 5 corrupted Airtable records to "Completed" with fields related to "status" or "Completed" (or similar) using airtable_update_records on table tblRelocations00001 for record IDs rece48bdecea1af4e, rec6ff7c1db59d44d, receee354b28a5d43, rec18494bda03df49, rec8086a371fb7a48.
OE 10: Post a message with content related to "ENG-220" or "backfill" or "retroactive" (or similar) to engineering channel (C003) using conversations_add_message summarizing that ENG-220's patch was forward-only, all 5 records across Vectral and Mosaic have been corrected, and a new Linear ticket tracks the backfill.
OE 11: Post a message with content related to "relocation" or "Completed" or "Vectral" or "Mosaic" (or similar) to #operations (C006) using conversations_add_message confirming all 5 relocation records are now Completed and identifying both affected clients.
OE 12: Reply to Rachel Whitfield using reply_to_email (parent: email_rachel_vectral_status_complaint) or send_email to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com with content related to "Completed" or "expense reimbursement" or "Terrence" or "Camille" or "Jordan" (or similar) confirming all three employees' records are now Completed and reimbursements can proceed.
OE 13: Notify Alejandro about QuickBooks billing conflicts on both affected clients using `send_email` covering INV-3042 and INV-3047 noting that both invoices were sent while Airtable still showed the moves as In Progress.
OE 14: Search the CRM using crm_search_companies or crm_list_contacts with a query related to "Mosaic" or "HR" (or similar) to find Tamara Byrd (contact_mosaic_hr, tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com), Mosaic Robotics HR Manager.
OE 15: Send an email using `send_email` to the Mosaic Robotics account manager, proactively notifying them that two of their employees had relocation records stuck at "In Progress" and that both have now been corrected to "Completed.".
OE 16: Check QuickBooks for Mosaic Robotics invoices using `quickbooks_search_invoices`, finding invoice INV-3047 for Andre Washington's Boston move.
OE 18: Check Chloe's calendar for upcoming Vectral or Mosaic calls using `calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to` or `calendar_search_events`, finding the March 20 Vectral bi-weekly sync call, and update that event's description using `calendar_edit_calendar_event` with a context note about the relocation status incident and resolution.

1 A new Linear issue was created (via `linear_create_issue`) tracking the MoveSync retroactive backfill or manual status correction for pre-patch records.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `linear_create_issue` with title 'MoveSync backfill: audit and correct all pre-March 12 corrupted relocation statuses', explicitly describing the retroactive gap and listing all 5 affected records. The issue was created under the Engineering team (ENG-398).
✓Run #2The agent called linear_create_issue with title 'Verify no additional MoveSync-corrupted records exist in historical data', which explicitly tracks retroactive backfill and auditing of pre-patch corrupted records. A second issue for patch validation was also created. Both are linked to the new Linear project.
✓Run #3The agent called `linear_create_issue` creating ENG-398 titled 'Verify MoveSync backfill completeness and confirm sync stability post-patch', with description explicitly noting the March 12 patch only prevented future overwrites and 5 records were already corrupted before it. This correctly tracks the retroactive backfill work.
✓Run #4Item 74 shows a `linear_create_issue` call with title 'Backfill corrupted relocation statuses and add regression monitoring', explicitly noting that ENG-397's March 12 patch did not backfill already-affected records and listing all 5 records.
✓Run #5The agent called `linear_create_issue` with title 'MoveSync backfill: audit all relocation records for status mismatches and add monitoring', which explicitly tracks the retroactive correction work (records not covered by the March 12 patch). The description includes all 5 record IDs and explains the gap.
✓Run #6The agent called `linear_create_issue` with title 'Backfill corrupted relocation statuses and verify MoveSync patch coverage', creating ENG-398 in the Engineering team. The issue description explicitly references the retroactive gap and all 5 affected records.

10 The engineering Slack message (visible in `conversations_add_message` parameters) references ENG-220 which explains the retroactive gap, noting that the patch applies to future runs and not to records corrupted before March 12.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #engineering Slack message explicitly states: 'the MoveSync patch from March 12 (ENG-397) stopped future status overwrites but did not fix records already corrupted before the patch date,' directly explaining the retroactive gap and why the 'done' fix was incomplete.
✓Run #2The #engineering Slack message explicitly states: 'the MoveSync sync bug fix deployed March 12 (ENG-397) successfully prevented future status overwrites, but it did not backfill records that were already corrupted before the patch.' This clearly explains the retroactive gap, even though the ticket is numbered ENG-397 in this system rather than ENG-220.

query construction
